description:
You are working as a database administrator inside a development VM.  
At the moment, all simulated firewall rules are kept in a plain-text file located at  
  /home/user/firewall.conf  

PostgreSQL is about to be enabled for query-tuning tests, so the TCP port 5432 must be opened in that rules file.

1. The file /home/user/firewall.conf already exists.  
   It currently ends with the line  
   “# DB RULES” (without the quotes) followed by a blank line and then the terminating line “COMMIT”.  
   NOTHING else in that file should be changed.

2. Using **exactly one single terminal command**, append a rule that allows inbound TCP traffic on port 5432.  
   The rule line must be spelled exactly as shown below (no extra spaces or comments):  
   -A INPUT -p tcp --dport 5432 -j ACCEPT

3. The very same command must also create a concise log of the change.  
   The log file must be called  
     /home/user/firewall_update.log  
   and, after the command finishes, it must contain exactly one line, with no leading or trailing spaces or extra blank lines:  
   5432/tcp ALLOW

Summary of what the automated checker will verify:
• /home/user/firewall.conf contains the new rule line “-A INPUT -p tcp --dport 5432 -j ACCEPT” somewhere **after** the marker line “# DB RULES” and **before** the terminating line “COMMIT”.  
• /home/user/firewall_update.log exists and its sole contents are exactly:  
  5432/tcp ALLOW  
  (terminated by a single Unix newline).  
• The task was completed with just one composite shell command (it may use pipes or ‘&&’, but must be submitted in one line).

No other output or files are required.  
Remember: you do NOT have root/sudo privileges, so limit yourself to file operations that a normal user can perform.
truth:
Initial state before the agent starts:

File: /home/user/firewall.conf  
Contents (each line shown exactly):
*filter
:INPUT DROP [0:0]
:FORWARD DROP [0:0]
:OUTPUT ACCEPT [0:0]

# Allow SSH
-A INPUT -p tcp --dport 22 -j ACCEPT

# DB RULES

COMMIT

Desired final state after successful completion:

1) /home/user/firewall.conf will be identical to the initial version except that one additional line
-A INPUT -p tcp --dport 5432 -j ACCEPT
appears on its own line, anywhere between “# DB RULES” and “COMMIT”; no other lines are altered.

2) /home/user/firewall_update.log exists with file mode 0644 (rw-r--r--) and contains exactly:
5432/tcp ALLOW\n  (Unix newline)

3) No other files are created or modified.

The checker will also capture the full shell history to confirm that only one command line (possibly containing pipes or logical operators) was used after the user prompt that performs both the file append and log creation.
name: task_000000_c84c194d
